text:
The Crohn’s Disease Exclusion Diet (CDED) is a therapeutic dietary strategy based on the exclusion of certain foods, replaced by specific foods combined with an enteral nutrition formula.
➔  Il is divided into several phases allowing a progressive reintroduction of foods while ensuring control of inflammation.
➔  In addition to the enteral nutrition formula, 5 foods are mandatory and other foods are allowed in defined quantities and frequencies. The list of allowed foods expands as one progresses through the phases of the CDED.
➔  Follow-up with professionals trained in this diet is essential.
